citations.narrative:
The dogs were individually examined shortly after their initial intake but did not have a complete physical exam in over 12
months from their initial exam. Thorough annual exams are needed to evaluate all of the dog's health and to provide
further treatment if indicated.
A complete physical examination from head to tail of each dog by the attending veterinarian not less than once every 12
months.
Correct by March 31, 2026, and forward.

The dogs were purchased fully vaccinated and were not given annual vaccinations as recommended in the written
program of veterinary care. There was no heartworm sampling being conducted as required by the AWA. "Dogs tested
only by request" as documented in the written program of veterinary care for dogs is not in accordance with a schedule for
heartworm testing.
Vaccinations for contagious and/or deadly diseases of dogs (including rabies, parvovirus and distemper) and sampling
and treatment of parasites and other pests (including fleas, worms, coccidia, giardia, and heartworm) in accordance with a
schedule approved by the attending veterinarian, unless otherwise required by a research protocol approved by the
Committee at research facilities.
Correct by March 31, 2026, and forward.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the Institutional Official.
n

narrative:
3.13(a)(2) 
   
Veterinary care for dogs. 
The dogs were individually examined shortly after their initial intake but did not have a complete physical exam in over 12 
months from their initial exam. Thorough annual exams are needed to evaluate all of the dog's health and to provide 
further treatment if indicated. 
 
A complete physical examination from head to tail of each dog by the attending veterinarian not less than once every 12 
months. 
 
Correct by March 31, 2026, and forward. 
3.13(a)(3) 
   
Veterinary care for dogs. 
The dogs were purchased fully vaccinated and were not given annual vaccinations as recommended in the written 
program of veterinary care. There was no heartworm sampling being conducted as required by the AWA. "Dogs tested 
only by request" as documented in the written program of veterinary care for dogs is not in accordance with a schedule for 
heartworm testing. 
 
Vaccinations for contagious and/or deadly diseases of dogs (including rabies, parvovirus and distemper) and sampling 
and treatment of parasites and other pests (including fleas, worms, coccidia, giardia, and heartworm) in accordance with a 
schedule approved by the attending veterinarian, unless otherwise required by a research protocol approved by the 
Committee at research facilities. 
 
Correct by March 31, 2026, and forward. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the Institutional Official.
